title: noccaea fendleri subsp. glauca
intro: Noccaea fendleri subsp. glauca, also known as Rocky Mountain pennyroyal, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural insect repellent and its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant is rich in essential oils, particularly limonene and camphor, which have been found to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting digestive health. Noccaea fendleri subsp. glauca is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, and can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
